title: Crisp Nutty Xanthic Uruguayan Padron Peppers Paella
description: A hearty, vegetarian paella dish from Uruguay, featuring crisp Padron peppers, nutty flavors, and a festive twist.

ingredients.name: Padron peppers
ingredients.notes: any variation of color is fine

ingredients.name: Smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: use high-quality smoked paprika for deep flavor

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: mince or press the garlic for optimal flavor

ingredients.name: Chickpeas
ingredients.notes: canned or cooked chickpeas work well

ingredients.name: Saffron Threads
ingredients.notes: use high-quality saffron for authentic flavor

ingredients.name: Tomato paste
ingredients.notes: use high-quality tomato paste for intense flavor

ingredients.name: White wine
ingredients.notes: dry white wine works best for this dish

ingredients.name: Vegetable broth
ingredients.notes: low-sodium or homemade broth is best

ingredients.name: Rice
ingredients.notes: short-grain rice or Calasparra rice is ideal for paella

ingredients.name: Cashews
ingredients.notes: toasted cashews add a nice crunch to the dish

ingredients.name: Sesame oil
ingredients.notes: use a light hand with sesame oil, as it's potent

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan or skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant and lightly golden.
Add the smoked paprika and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chickpeas and cook for 2-3 minutes, until lightly browned.
Add the Padron peppers and cook for 3-4 minutes, until slightly charred and tender.
Add the tomato paste and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Add the white wine and vegetable broth, and bring to a simmer.
Add the saffron threads and cook for 2-3 minutes, until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Add the rice to the pan and stir to coat with the flavorful liquid.
Transfer the pan to a preheated sous-vide water bath at 180°F (82°C) for 30 minutes.
Remove the pan from the sous-vide and stir in the toasted cashews and a drizzle of sesame oil.
Serve the paella immediately, garnished with additional Padron peppers if desired.
